perturbation.original_text: 7. Sensitive Information . To the extent that any Evaluation Material may include extremely sensitive material or information of Company relating to pricing information, prospective client lists, patent information, trade secrets, or source code that is identified by the Company in writing as sensitive ("Sensitive Information”), you understand and agree that in addition to all of the restrictions applying to Evaluation Material you shall comply with the additional restrictions outlined in this Section 7. For purposes of Sensitive Information, each Representative must also be approved by the Company prior to receiving such Sensitive Information. Company will determine, in its sole discretion, whether the proposed Representative is able to exploit the Sensitive Information commercially. Representatives are not authorized to further disclose such Sensitive Information to any other Representative without prior authorization from Company. Furthermore, for certain components of Sensitive Information Company may have additional specific data security procedures, requirements and instructions to which the you or your Representatives must comply prior to receiving or using such Sensitive Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Confidential Data. To the extent that any Sensitive Information may include extremely sensitive material or information of Company relating to pricing information, prospective client lists, patent information, trade secrets, or source code that is identified by the Company in writing as sensitive ("Sensitive Information”), you understand and agree that in addition to all of the restrictions applying to Evaluation Material you shall comply with the additional restrictions outlined in this Section 7. For purposes of Sensitive Information, each Representative must also be approved by the Company prior to receiving such Sensitive Information. Company will determine, in its sole discretion, whether the proposed Representative is able to exploit the Sensitive Information commercially. Representatives are not authorized to further disclose such Sensitive Information to any other Representative without prior authorization from Company. Furthermore, for certain components of Sensitive Information Company may have additional specific data security procedures, requirements and instructions to which the you or your Representatives must comply prior to receiving or using such Sensitive Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Material' to 'Sensitive Information' at the beginning of Section 7, while keeping 'Evaluation Material' later in the same paragraph, creates ambiguity about what the additional restrictions apply to. The first sentence now references both 'Sensitive Information' and 'Evaluation Material,' without clarifying their relationship. This leads to a contradiction within the same section. It remains unclear whether the 'Sensitive Information' mentioned at the beginning encompasses all 'Evaluation Material'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Confidential Data. To the extent that any Sensitive Information may include extremely sensitive material or information of Company relating to pricing information, prospective client lists, patent information, trade secrets, or source code that is identified by the Company in writing as sensitive ("Sensitive Information”), you understand and agree that in addition to all of the restrictions applying to Evaluation Material you shall comply with the additional restrictions outlined in this Section 7. For purposes of Sensitive Information, each Representative must also be approved by the Company prior to receiving such Sensitive Information. Company will determine, in its sole discretion, whether the proposed Representative is able to exploit the Sensitive Information commercially. Representatives are not authorized to further disclose such Sensitive Information to any other Representative without prior authorization from Company. Furthermore, for certain components of Sensitive Information Company may have additional specific data security procedures, requirements and instructions to which the you or your Representatives must comply prior to receiving or using such Sensitive Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the first sentence of Section 7 from referencing 'Evaluation Material' to 'Sensitive Information', while the same sentence later still refers to 'Evaluation Material'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear whether the restrictions outlined in Section 7 apply to 'Evaluation Material' independently of 'Sensitive Information' or if 'Evaluation Material' is now considered a subset of 'Sensitive Information'. This ambiguity regarding the scope of the restrictions based on the change in terminology constitutes a misaligned terminology in-text contradiction, making it difficult to determine which terms apply and causing confusion.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . This letter agreement shall be in effect for a period of one year from the date hereof. Until a definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed by the parties, neither party shall be under any legal obligation or have any liability to the other party of any nature whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise (other than with respect to the confidentiality and other matters set forth herein). Each party may, in its sole discretion, terminate discussions and negotiations with the other party at any time and for any reason. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly deliver to the disclosing party any and all Evaluation Material (and all copies thereof and extracts therefrom, whether in hard-copy form or intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party. In the event of such a decision or request, all Evaluation Material prepared by the receiving party shall be destroyed and no copy thereof shall be retained, the receiving party shall provide written confirmation of such destruction. In no event shall either party be obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material prepared by it or its Representatives to the other party. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality, non-use and other obligations hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . This letter agreement shall be in effect for a period of one year from the date hereof. Until a definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed by the parties, neither party shall be under any legal obligation or have any liability to the other party of any nature whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise (other than with respect to the confidentiality and other matters set forth herein). Each party may, in its sole discretion, terminate discussions and negotiations with the other party at any time and for any reason. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly deliver to the disclosing party any and all Protected Data (and all copies thereof and extracts therefrom, whether in hard-copy form or intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party. In the event of such a decision or request, all Evaluation Material prepared by the receiving party shall be destroyed and no copy thereof shall be retained, the receiving party shall provide written confirmation of such destruction. In no event shall either party be obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material prepared by it or its Representatives to the other party. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality, non-use and other obligations hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Evaluation Material” to “Protected Data” in the first instance in Section 8, but reverting to “Evaluation Material” later in the same section, creates a clear contradiction. The receiving party is now obligated to return "Protected Data", but the instructions for what to do with the material and the enduring obligations relate to "Evaluation Material". If “Protected Data” is not the same as “Evaluation Material”, it's uncertain what should happen with it. Section 8 contains this in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Termination of Discussions . This letter agreement shall be in effect for a period of one year from the date hereof. Until a definitive agreement regarding the Transaction has been executed by the parties, neither party shall be under any legal obligation or have any liability to the other party of any nature whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise (other than with respect to the confidentiality and other matters set forth herein). Each party may, in its sole discretion, terminate discussions and negotiations with the other party at any time and for any reason. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving a written notice of termination. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly deliver to the disclosing party any and all Protected Data (and all copies thereof and extracts therefrom, whether in hard-copy form or intangible media, such as electronic mail or computer files) furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party. In the event of such a decision or request, all Evaluation Material prepared by the receiving party shall be destroyed and no copy thereof shall be retained, the receiving party shall provide written confirmation of such destruction. In no event shall either party be obligated to disclose or provide the Evaluation Material prepared by it or its Representatives to the other party. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality, non-use and other obligations hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Evaluation Material" with "Protected Data" in one instance within Section 8, while later instances in the same section revert to using "Evaluation Material". The Contradicted Text is exactly the same as the changed text, meaning that within the same Section 8, the two terms are used, creating a contradiction of which definition is valid and creating conflicting obligations. This contradicts itself and meets the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Evaluation Material” to “Confidential Information” in Section 11's consideration clause makes it unclear whether the non-solicitation agreement is in exchange for all confidential information or just the specifically defined 'Evaluation Material.' The non-solicitation clause refers to people involved in 'discussions hereunder,' which are related to the Evaluation Material per the contract's preamble. This change implies it could be any confidential information at all, widening the scope unexpectedly. The in-text contradiction takes place in Section 11, where the scope of what's being considered is changed mid-paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Non-Solicitation . In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to you, each party agrees that for a period of one year from the date set forth above, without the other party’s prior written consent, neither party nor its affiliates will solicit, directly or indirectly, to employ any person who is now employed by the other party or its affiliates and is directly involved in the discussions hereunder (a “Covered Person”). The term “solicit to employ” shall not be deemed to include: (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically directed toward employees of the other party, (ii) an event in which such Covered Person contacts the such party or its Representatives on the Covered Person’s own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from such party or its Representatives, or (iii) such Covered Person is referred to such party by search firms, employment agencies, or other similar entities, provided that such entities have not been specifically instructed by such party to solicit the employees of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Evaluation Material" to "Confidential Information" broadens the scope of the consideration in the non-solicitation agreement. The original text links the non-solicitation clause to discussions about the "Evaluation Material." The changed text potentially applies the non-solicitation clause to any confidential information exchanged, which is a different and much broader scope than intended by the original contract. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'discussions hereunder,' which are specific to the 'Evaluation Material', now potentially broadened to any confidential information by the change.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning confidentiality of their respective Evaluation Material, and no modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall be binding upon either party hereto, unless approved in writing by each such party.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Entire Agreement . This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning confidentiality of their respective Data, and no modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall be binding upon either party hereto, unless approved in writing by each such party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Evaluation Material” to “Data” in the entire agreement clause (Section 17) significantly alters the scope of the agreement. If other sections of the agreement still refer to 'Evaluation Material,' and other definitions have been altered, it's now uncertain what the 'entire agreement' actually covers. This creates a fundamental contradiction because it's unclear whether the agreement covers all data, or only the specifically defined 'Evaluation Material' (which may or may not encompass the changed terms 'Protected Information', 'Confidential Data' etc.) The term 'Data' now contradicts with references to 'Evaluation Material' in other sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Evaluation Material . The term “Evaluation Material” shall be deemed to include all information, records, notes, analyses, compilations, studies, reports, projections, forecasts, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives that contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto, which is not available to the general public. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives, (ii) was within the receiving party’s possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with (or subject to any other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to) the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with (or subject to any other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to) the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information or (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of Evaluation Material provided such independent development can reasonably be proven by contemporaneous written records. For the avoidance of doubt, the failure by the disclosing party to include any legend or other marking of the Evaluation Material as confidential or proprietary shall not be dispositive as to whether such information is Evaluation Material, provided that such information should reasonably be deemed by its nature to be confidential.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Evaluation Material" to "Data" in Section 17, the entire agreement clause, directly contradicts the definition of "Evaluation Material" in Section 1. Section 17 now refers to all "Data," while Section 1 defines a specific subset called "Evaluation Material." This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the entire agreement covers just the specifically defined "Evaluation Material" or all data, making the scope of the agreement ambiguous and inconsistent.
